summary: Remote-eval backdoor disguised as SVG sanitizer
details: swift-parse-stream advertises itself as an SVG fetcher/sanitizer but its exported getPlugin() function fetches https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/NGY3C — an anonymous, mutable JSON paste host — and passes the response's `model` field directly to eval(). Errors in both the HTTP request and JSON.parse paths are silently swallowed via empty catch blocks, concealing the loader from casual debugging. Because jsonkeeper.com pastes can be edited at any time by whoever holds the paste, the package author (or anyone who has captured the paste id) can ship arbitrary JavaScript to every consumer that invokes getPlugin(). The ~80 lines of plausible SVG sanitization code surrounding this loader function as a cover story. This is a classic remote-loader backdoor pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
